description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and dark, contrasting with the lighter, stone-colored ridges between them. The block has a solid, robust appearance, with clean, straight edges and a uniform thickness. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, emphasizing the striking pattern on the top. The coloration suggests a material like granite or concrete, with the grooves possibly serving a functional or decorative purpose. This type of block is commonly used in urban environments as a barrier or obstacle, often found in plazas, sidewalks, or as part of landscape architecture.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a bold striped pattern. The block is solid, with smooth sides and a robust, urban appearance.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the front, showing the full width of the striped grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Viewed from the side, the grooves appear to run vertically, emphasizing the depth and spacing of the pattern.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, with the same horizontal striped grooves visible across the top surface.
description_view_3: From the opposite side, the grooves again run vertically, matching the pattern seen in view 1.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surfaces appear clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
